basic_details.job_title: BEL Bharat Electronics Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 09 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) has invited online applications for 9 posts of Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) at its Chennai Unit through Advertisement No. 4788/002/REC/HR&A/CHN/2026 dated 17 June 2026. Candidates with a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering degree in Mechanical Engineering, aged up to 28 years as on 01 June 2026, can apply online until 01 July 2026. Selection is through a 100-mark written test, and selected candidates start at Rs. 30,000 per month.

application_fee.details: Candidates wondering about the cost of applying can note that General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay Rs. 177 (Rs. 150 application fee plus 18% GST) through the SBI Collect payment gateway. SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are fully exempted from paying this application fee. The fee, once paid, is non-refundable under any circumstance, and applications submitted without the prescribed fee (where applicable) will not be considered.

age_limit.details: Candidates asking who can apply for BEL Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) should know that a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized University or Institution is the minimum requirement, and no post-qualification work experience is needed. Only the Mechanical Engineering branch is treated as relevant for this post, and if the specialization printed on a candidate's degree certificate does not match the branch selected in the online application, that application will not be considered for selection. The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxations available for reserved categories. | Educational qualification requirements include: | B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engg in Mechanical Engineering (4-year course), pass class, from a recognized university. Candidates must possess all semester/year mark sheets along with the final degree certificate. A CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate is required wherever the marksheet uses CGPA grading. | Age relaxation as on 01 June 2026: | ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (effective limit: 33 years). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (effective limit: 31 years). PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability get an additional 10 years relaxation on top of the relaxation applicable to their category. SSLC/SSC/ISC marks card or any other valid document is accepted as proof of date of birth.

qualification.details: Candidates asking who can apply for BEL Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) should know that a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized University or Institution is the minimum requirement, and no post-qualification work experience is needed. Only the Mechanical Engineering branch is treated as relevant for this post, and if the specialization printed on a candidate's degree certificate does not match the branch selected in the online application, that application will not be considered for selection. The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxations available for reserved categories. | Educational qualification requirements include: | B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engg in Mechanical Engineering (4-year course), pass class, from a recognized university. Candidates must possess all semester/year mark sheets along with the final degree certificate. A CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate is required wherever the marksheet uses CGPA grading. | Age relaxation as on 01 June 2026: | ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (effective limit: 33 years). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (effective limit: 31 years). PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability get an additional 10 years relaxation on top of the relaxation applicable to their category. SSLC/SSC/ISC marks card or any other valid document is accepted as proof of date of birth.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking about the pay for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) can note that BEL offers a consolidated monthly remuneration of Rs. 30,000 in the 1st year, Rs. 35,000 in the 2nd year, and Rs. 40,000 in the 3rd year if the contract is extended. In addition, BEL pays a consolidated Rs. 12,000 per year toward insurance premium, attire allowance, stitching charges, and footwear allowance, released in two installments - the first with the first month's salary and the second after completing 6 months of service. | Engagement terms include: | Initial engagement period: 2 years, extendable by up to 1 year (maximum tenure of 3 years) based on project requirement and performance. Insurance coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for medical cover and Rs. 5 lakh for life cover must be arranged by the Trainee Engineer at the time of joining, with the policy copy submitted to HR; renewal and premium payment remain the individual's responsibility throughout the tenure. | Advancement to Project Engineer-I: After completing 2 years as Trainee Engineer, candidates become eligible for advancement to Project Engineer-I if they score 80% or above (aggregate) in Performance Assessment for both years, maintain attendance above 80% in the preceding year with good general discipline, and have no adverse remarks in the preceding 2 years. Candidates who do not meet these criteria continue as Trainee Engineer-III for one additional year, after which the engagement ends. Once advanced, Project Engineer-I is engaged for 3 years, extendable by up to 1 year based on project requirement and performance. | Retention Bonus for Project Engineers: Project Engineers who have advanced from the Trainee Engineer role are entitled to a Retention Bonus of Rs. 25,000 for every completed year, provided they complete an overall tenure of 4 years in the Project Engineer role. This bonus is calculated for the Project Engineer tenure only and is paid as a lump sum of Rs. 1,00,000 at the end of 4 years.

and final selection follows merit order calculated category-wise and discipline-wise. The minimum qualifying marks differ by category: General

and the written test will be conducted in Chennai. Separately

the notification mentions that candidates currently working on a permanent or fixed-tenure basis in PSUs or Government organizations must produce a No Objection Certificate "at the time of the interview
" which indicates there may be a verification or interview stage for such candidates after the written test.
As a general industry pattern (not stated in the official notification)
PSU recruitment of this kind typically takes 6–10 weeks from the application closing date to the written test
followed by another 4–6 weeks for results and document verification. Candidates should treat this only as a rough planning estimate and rely on the official BEL website for the actual schedule.
how_to_apply: Candidates ready to apply should know that applications are accepted only through the online mode, and no manual, paper, or hard copy applications will be entertained under any circumstances. Before starting the form, candidates should keep all required documents, photographs, and the application fee payment ready, since changes are not permitted once the form is submitted.
Read the eligibility criteria and confirm you meet the qualification, branch, and age requirements.
Fill in all details accurately in the online application form, since edits are not allowed post-submission.
Pay the application fee of Rs. 177 via the SBI Collect link (skip this step if you belong to SC/ST/PwBD).
Note down the SBI Collect Reference Number generated after payment and enter it in the application form.
Upload all mandatory documents, including educational certificates, category certificates, and ID proof.
Verify the entire form carefully and submit it before 01 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: Quick Summary: BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates looking for a one-glance overview of this recruitment can refer to the table below, which captures the post name, organization, total vacancies, application deadline, and mode of application. This is the fastest way to confirm whether you are eligible to proceed with a detailed reading of the notification.

rows.details: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the key dates for this recruitment can note that the notification was released on 17 June 2026, and the online application window closes on 01 July 2026. Age eligibility is calculated as on 01 June 2026, which is the reference date used for all age-related calculations, including relaxations. The notification does not specify a separate written test date, so candidates should keep checking the official BEL website for updates after the application deadline.

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Breakdown: Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical), Chennai
content_sections.text: Out of the 9 total vacancies advertised for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical), the seats are distributed across reservation categories as per Government of India norms. Candidates applying under OBC-NCL, EWS, or ST categories should apply against their respective category code rather than the unreserved (UR) quota. The table below gives the exact category-wise split.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria: Qualification and Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates asking who can apply for BEL Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) should know that a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized University or Institution is the minimum requirement, and no post-qualification work experience is needed. Only the Mechanical Engineering branch is treated as relevant for this post, and if the specialization printed on a candidate's degree certificate does not match the branch selected in the online application, that application will not be considered for selection. The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxations available for reserved categories. Educational qualification requirements include: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engg in Mechanical Engineering (4-year course), pass class, from a recognized university. Candidates must possess all semester/year mark sheets along with the final degree certificate. A CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate is required wherever the marksheet uses CGPA grading. Age relaxation as on 01 June 2026: ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (effective limit: 33 years). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (effective limit: 31 years). PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability get an additional 10 years relaxation on top of the relaxation applicable to their category. SSLC/SSC/ISC marks card or any other valid document is accepted as proof of date of birth.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the cost of applying can note that General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay Rs. 177 (Rs. 150 application fee plus 18% GST) through the SBI Collect payment gateway. SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are fully exempted from paying this application fee. The fee, once paid, is non-refundable under any circumstance, and applications submitted without the prescribed fee (where applicable) will not be considered.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical)
content_sections.text: Candidates asking how they will be selected can note that BEL shortlists eligible applicants for a written test carrying 100 marks, and final selection follows merit order calculated category-wise and discipline-wise. The minimum qualifying marks differ by category: General, EWS, and OBC-NCL candidates need 35%, while ST and PwBD candidates need 30% to be considered suitable. The question paper carries negative marking of 1/4 mark for every wrong response, so candidates should attempt questions carefully, and the written test will be conducted in Chennai. Separately, the notification mentions that candidates currently working on a permanent or fixed-tenure basis in PSUs or Government organizations must produce a No Objection Certificate "at the time of the interview," which indicates there may be a verification or interview stage for such candidates after the written test. As a general industry pattern (not stated in the official notification), PSU recruitment of this kind typically takes 6–10 weeks from the application closing date to the written test, followed by another 4–6 weeks for results and document verification. Candidates should treat this only as a rough planning estimate and rely on the official BEL website for the actual schedule.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates ready to apply should know that applications are accepted only through the online mode, and no manual, paper, or hard copy applications will be entertained under any circumstances. Before starting the form, candidates should keep all required documents, photographs, and the application fee payment ready, since changes are not permitted once the form is submitted. Visit the official application link: https://jobapply.in/BEL2026ChennaiEATTechTE . Read the eligibility criteria and confirm you meet the qualification, branch, and age requirements. Fill in all details accurately in the online application form, since edits are not allowed post-submission. Pay the application fee of Rs. 177 via the SBI Collect link (skip this step if you belong to SC/ST/PwBD). Note down the SBI Collect Reference Number generated after payment and enter it in the application form. Upload all mandatory documents, including educational certificates, category certificates, and ID proof. Verify the entire form carefully and submit it before 01 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: Salary, Pay Structure, and Career Growth
content_sections.text: Candidates asking about the pay for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) can note that BEL offers a consolidated monthly remuneration of Rs. 30,000 in the 1st year, Rs. 35,000 in the 2nd year, and Rs. 40,000 in the 3rd year if the contract is extended. In addition, BEL pays a consolidated Rs. 12,000 per year toward insurance premium, attire allowance, stitching charges, and footwear allowance, released in two installments - the first with the first month's salary and the second after completing 6 months of service. Engagement terms include: Initial engagement period: 2 years, extendable by up to 1 year (maximum tenure of 3 years) based on project requirement and performance. Insurance coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for medical cover and Rs. 5 lakh for life cover must be arranged by the Trainee Engineer at the time of joining, with the policy copy submitted to HR; renewal and premium payment remain the individual's responsibility throughout the tenure. Advancement to Project Engineer-I: After completing 2 years as Trainee Engineer, candidates become eligible for advancement to Project Engineer-I if they score 80% or above (aggregate) in Performance Assessment for both years, maintain attendance above 80% in the preceding year with good general discipline, and have no adverse remarks in the preceding 2 years. Candidates who do not meet these criteria continue as Trainee Engineer-III for one additional year, after which the engagement ends. Once advanced, Project Engineer-I is engaged for 3 years, extendable by up to 1 year based on project requirement and performance. Retention Bonus for Project Engineers: Project Engineers who have advanced from the Trainee Engineer role are entitled to a Retention Bonus of Rs. 25,000 for every completed year, provided they complete an overall tenure of 4 years in the Project Engineer role. This bonus is calculated for the Project Engineer tenure only and is paid as a lump sum of Rs. 1,00,000 at the end of 4 years.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for BEL Trainee Engineer Application
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing their application should gather the following documents in advance, since incomplete uploads can lead to rejection at the scrutiny stage: SSLC/SSC/10th standard marks card or any other valid document as proof of date of birth. B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering degree certificate clearly indicating the discipline and class obtained. All semester marks cards or the consolidated marks card. CGPA conversion certificate, wherever applicable. Caste/Tribe/Community/Disability/Economic status certificate for candidates claiming SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD/EWS reservation - OBC (NCL) certificates must be issued on or after 01 June 2025, and EWS income & asset certificates must be for 2025-26 or the current financial year. For candidates with post-qualification work experience: a service certificate from the previous/current employer, or alternatively the offer of current appointment, employee ID proof, and latest pay slip together. SBI Collect payment challan (fee receipt). Identity proof such as Aadhaar Card, Driving License, or Voter ID.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying
content_sections.text: Many candidates lose their chance at selection due to avoidable errors rather than a lack of eligibility, so it helps to double-check these points before hitting submit. Reviewing this checklist can save candidates from disqualification at the document verification stage, which is a common pain point in PSU recruitment drives. Branch mismatch: Applying under "Mechanical" when the degree certificate shows a different specialization will lead to automatic rejection, since BEL only accepts the exact branch printed on the degree certificate. Skipping the SBI Collect Reference Number: Forgetting to enter this number in the application form (for fee-paying categories) can make the application incomplete. Using someone else's email ID: Since all future communication from BEL happens only through email, using a friend's or relative's email address risks missing important updates. Lateral applications by existing BEL Trainee Engineers: Candidates currently working as Trainee Engineers in any BEL unit are not eligible to apply for this post, and such applications are summarily rejected. Submitting without proper category certificates: OBC-NCL and EWS certificates have specific validity date requirements; an outdated certificate can disqualify an otherwise eligible candidate. Assuming edits are possible later: Since the portal does not allow changes after submission, candidates should review every field carefully before the final submit.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 01 July 2026. No applications will be accepted after this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical)?
faq.answer: There are 9 total vacancies, split as UR-3, EWS-2, OBC-3, and ST-1.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required?
faq.answer: Candidates need a B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc Engineering (4-year course) in Mechanical Engineering with pass class from a recognized university; no work experience is required.

faq.question: Q4. What is the upper age limit for this post?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 28 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxation of 5 years for ST, 3 years for OBC, and an additional 10 years for PwBD candidates with 40% or more disability.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for BEL Trainee Engineer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: General, EWS, and OBC candidates pay Rs. 177, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a 100-mark written test held in Chennai, with qualifying marks of 35% for General/EWS/OBC-NCL and 30% for ST/PwBD, and negative marking of 1/4 mark per wrong answer.

faq.question: Q7. What salary will a Trainee Engineer-I (Mechanical) receive?
faq.answer: The pay is Rs. 30,000 per month in year 1, Rs. 35,000 in year 2, and Rs. 40,000 in year 3 (if extended), plus a consolidated Rs. 12,000 per year for allowances.
